content:
Hi @Danilo Krummrich @Alexandre Courbot @Dave Airlie

I'm looking to pick up my first task in the nova-core subsystem and was looking at the "CE engine" item in todo.rst (Support for the copy engine / GSP message interface).

I've spent some time looking into the codebase to understand the GSP boot flow and the Cmdq ring buffers. I noticed the C-struct bindings for the CE engines (like AMPERE_DMA_COPY_A etc.) are already present in the firmware headers, but the Rust wrappers, module, and RPC commands haven't been implemented yet.

Before I dive in and start wiring up the RPCs and the engine lifecycle, I wanted to quickly check: is anyone currently working on this, or is the runway clear for me to take a stab at it?

Thanks!

(We should probably move this thread to the Nova channel?)

Thanks for your interest! There is still some plumbing required before we can consider working on the CE engine (we are still in the early stages), and we have someone internally looking into that already.

Generally speaking I'd encourage people who want to get involved to start by providing code reviews to get familiar with the driver. I don't know your background but if you are new to kernel development then the larger R4L might be a better area to get started - Nova (especially at the current stage) is not very accessible without some strong domain-specific background.
This topic was moved here from #General > Nova Copy Engine (CE) TODO by Miguel Ojeda.

@Alexandre Courbot Not entirely new around here. I have already contributed a few patch series, mm related. Waiting for them to be accepted in Linus's branch. I assume they would be added in the next Minor Release. Likewise, I wanted to be involved in the GPU side of things as well, and saw the DRM and Nova.
I understand that it is under development, but, other than reviewing the code, are there any other materials I can look at?
I saw your submissions. To be completely transparent, working on Nova at this stage is considerably more involved and requires some GPU background and understanding of their internals.
Didn't see this until now but I have been working on CE support locally and have it basically working already. Thank you for your interest though!
